reviews.review_msg: Struggled at first but Dr. Warriner gave me some great advice on how to write a philosophy paper and I nailed the remaining papers. Her lectures can be tricky to follow but that was the nature of the class, but she was very conscious of backtracking and making sure her point was presented clearly. Would definitely take her class again.

Jennifer was an amazing professor! She really made sure to establish a safe learning environment and include everyone's opinions, as well as make herself available outside of class hours to help explain concepts and paper topics. Her grading is very fair and her feedback is insanely detailed. Lectures also aren't mandatory but are super helpful!

I took PHIL 321 and 326 with Dr. Warriner and I enjoyed both classes. Specifically, it's nice to have a phil prof with real world experiences, and on top of that she was also an excellent teacher and fair marker. I would definitely take a class with her again.

Took bioethics with her and loved it!! Very good at carrying class discussions and is accessible for feedback and help. Slightly confused by her grading as I did really well on some essays and others not so much even though I applied her feedback before submitting them all. Very passionate and knowledgable and I looked forward to class every week!
She is amazing. Made me want to do a minor in philosophy. Course is heavily curved. If you want to do well make sure you really understand the concepts, go to all tutorials and ask questions!

The MC on the midterm and finals were very tricky but the short answer's were easy. The TA's gave low marks to people on the papers, and never really taught us how to write a good philosophy paper. They always kept telling us that a philosophy paper is different from all the papers you've ever wrote. So, shouldn't they teach you it first?

Jen is a good prof. This was not my first Phil course,I aced 105 and 100W but I have no idea how people (if any) got an A in this class. The average was 72 and was curved to become a B- for everyone. The TA's marking seemed to clump everyone together. I had no idea how to ace assignments or tests and "the right way" was never explained. Frustrating

No group work, records lectures, tries to be as thorough as possible in the 50 minutes given. Lectures are well thought out, her teaching ability is amazing if you pay attention. Gives the general gist of what the philosophers are talking about, but honestly your grade will mostly depend on which TA you get. Get Anthony if you can.

Records lectures which is nice. Goes over everything you need to know about the readings and only tests from what she talks about in lecture, but being familiar with the readings is a must. Get Anthony as your TA, he's the nicest guy and incredibly helpful, and I would not have done as well without him.
She only has 30 minute office hours and that shows how little care she has for her students. Her midterms are extremely impractical and they test you on the most pointless topics.
HER SLIDES ARE AWFUL!!!!!!!!! I studied by myself the whole course. Terrible professor. Avoid her if you can!!!
Terrible professor do not recommend. Avoid her if you can.

Dr. Warriner is really nice and gives fascinating lectures. She encourages questions and gives helpful and in-depth answers. The class is quite difficult, though, and it's important to actual understand the material rather than just memorizing the slides for the exams.

Dr. Warriner is amazing. She is very accessible for help and gives in-depth responses, even over email if you are unable to make her office hours. The two papers written in this class are fairly graded as well. However, although the content is interesting, prepare for it to be difficult. If it wasn't for the prof, I would not of enjoyed the class

Recommended if you need a Phil or W class. She's great if you're new to philosophy. Unbiased. Will help you develop your position, whatever it is. Class is about interesting & important modern moral issues. Explains readings clearly. Don't memorize slides, understand them and be able to explain philosophers' positions. Note: gl with TAs :/
BEST OF THE BEST! if one is serious to follow up her lecture that it. you have what u want. I would definitely take another class with her.

Prof. Warriner's lectures were well paced and she definitely enjoyed teaching the class. The class itself was very interesting in terms of the topics we covered. There were readings that definitely help you understand the material better, but not necessary to do well in the class. Show up to class, study the lecture slides, and you'll be fine.

Extremely well-paced and thorough lecturer - her slides are clear and she speaks about complicated issues in an accessible way. Charming sense of humour and reasonably difficult standards in exams. There is a lot of reading to do for the class, but her slides cover all of the materials on the tests, so readings are only necessary for writing papers
Made a nice philosophy course that is usually about Aristotle and various normative ethics into a course about abortion and violent fap material, fabulous

I enjoyed this class. The ideas were relevant and interesting.  She would go over the readings in class and help make them understandable, which was extremely helpful and why I suggest you attend lecture.  Memorization isn't useful most of the time, you need to understand the material

Lots of readings but if you attend lecture and pay attention to her notes you don't actually have to do the readings because she goes over all the main points in the reading. Have to put the effort in the papers for a good mark, midterm is hard if you don't study well. Very helpful in office hours, so asks questions then!

She is a sweet professor. Great explanations and even if you don't do the reading, her lectures help. The TA is absolutely terrible though - if you get Aaron, drop it immediately. He rants too much and just wants to waste time for the sake of it

Had her for Phil 120 and 221. I love her organization. Some philosophy profs have a tendency to get sidetracked in rambles but Jenntypically stays focused. One of my favourite Phil profs. And she's hot.

Took what could have been an awesome class and made it incredibly boring. Her teaching style is dry and hard to stay focused on. We were told multiple times that writing a philosophy paper is different than regular papers yet were not told how to write a good one and then received incredibly low grades. Wish this class had a better prof.

Dr. Warriner is a professional, competent teacher. I felt that the course material she put together was interesting and fit for the course level. She was patient with questions and handled them well. This course may not be for those not particularly interested in philosophy. Overall I thought her a fair marker and clear lecturer.
I am not sure why her rating is high, but she was beyond horrible.
The worst professor I have ever had. I have never ever seen any rude, mean teacher like this

Be prepared to waste valuable time and money. If this is a required course for you -- be forewarned and take it with someone (anyone) else. She's a complicated one.  Unless you have the time and effort to distract yourself from your studies, run for the hills. But, if you happen to get trapped in her class, lay low and you should get by unscathed.
I had high hopes and was disappointed
Avoid.
Stay away from this class. The tests are impossible, avg was in the 50's. I am a good student and barely walked away with a c-. Def a gpa killer.

Jennifer is easily one of the best professors! I told her I was extremely nervous about philosophy and she was consistently there for support, making it one of the most enjoyable classes! I highly recommend taking a class with her for the experience alone, especially smaller classes so you can get to know what an amazing person she is first hand!
One of the best profs by far at SFU. She knows what's she's talking about and will give you that extra help that you need if you're not clear on a certain theory. Highly recommended.

I'm half way through my third year and I would say she is possibly the best professor I've had at SFU! The readings are alright but sometimes a little boring yet she somehow manages to make every lecture extremely interesting! Exams are comprehensive but manageable if you understand everything. I would recommend her a million times over!!!

Jennifer is one of the best profs I have come in contact with so far. Her lectures are never boring and easy to follow. There was always one student who would argue theories, but never once did she get frustrated. She gave you all the readings online, but they weren't necessary because she explained everything so clearly. She made philosophy fun
Lectures are informative. Material was straightforward and comprehensible. Prof was kinda clear with grading criteria. Sometimes you don't know what she's expecting, but the course is easy to pass.

no textbook, all the readings are on canvas.  lectures are really helpful for understanding the readings.  short papers and easy tutorial participation marks. midterm and final both had very tricky multiple choice

An angel of a professor. We had a very annoying student in our course who would literally look up arguments against the papers she assigned and read them off his computer in class. She never once lost her temper and treated everyone always professionally and fairly. She is very dedicated, always ready to help out a student.
Won't give you an A+ unless your paper is "publishable."  Hasn't published many papers herself...

I absolutely love Jennifer . She is the reason I took the second class with her and if I ever see any other phil classes with her I would take without hesitations. She is super supportive, her classes are interesting and she is very nice. She is not an easy marker though but is a fair marker I think because its always harder to get marks in phil
Best phil prof at SFU. extremely helpful, makes everything you need to know very clear, and makes the subject interesting.
Dr. Warriner is AWESOME! I went to every class simply because I love her class!She is really supportive, strongly recommended!

My fellow students, if you are looking for a life-time mentor, seeking for a professor who might strengthen your critical thinking process, don't miss the opportunity to take one of Dr. Warriner's courses. You will not regret your choice forever!

I'm a huge fan of Dr. Warriner. I went to her office hours almost every week simply because I really, really talking to her. She was hugely supportive when I needed some pushing in the right direction for my assignments. She didn't favor people at all. I loved this class, as you can probably tell :)

Oddly specific paper guidelines that impair ability to learn how to write. Chose a very high value exam question on a very obscure point in the argument she chose. The phil part seemed tacked on to the lit. I had high hopes and was disappointed.

Nice teacher, she makes the material, is very available for help and gives you tons of helpful comments on papers. Her marking is fair. She encouraged discussion in the class. Only issue was that sometimes that discussion detracted from my knowledge (ie; super specific student questions that she wouldn't nip in the bud) I highly re commend her!
no comments
Great professor. She is very friendly and kind professor. I went to see her today, she even offers me help whenever I needed. A big thank to her.

good prof! midterm is fair but the average is low. Final is lengthy..Low stake assignments do not require a lot of efforts. the only problem is that she does not share her notes. Take a laptop to the class. Notes is extremly important for doing well in the exams. BTW tutorial can be boring. Prof hold some of the tut as well.

Mid-term was quite easy...however the final was much harder.  Most of the material she says is based from her notes but the final didn't seem that way.  Overall she is a good teacher though, very nice, very helpful.

Knowledgeable Prof. She explains everything in detail. Her class is engaging that she answers students' questions in class. Tests are based on her notes. TA marks everything for her so keep a good relationship with your TA. You don't wanna skip a single class because she is hot and her voice is sweat :)

Nothing to complain about, except for the fact that she doesn't have regular office hours. However, she replies promptly to emails and will answer your questions directly in class. Oh, and she sticks to EARLY modern philosophy even though the course description was modern philosophy. Not a biggie, though.
Friendly teacher ever!But nothing provided on webct..no notes, slides, records..
Awesome
